_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Avoid force-unwrapping `UserDefaults` in production source.**

Line 29 can crash if suite creation fails. Guard it and fail with a clear error path instead.

 

<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
 `@MainActor`
 func runDump() {
-    let defaults = UserDefaults(suiteName: "tnf.a11y.dump")!
+    guard let defaults = UserDefaults(suiteName: "tnf.a11y.dump") else {
+        FileHandle.standardError.write(Data("Failed: could not create UserDefaults suite tnf.a11y.dump\n".utf8))
+        exit(1)
+    }
     defaults.removePersistentDomain(forName: "tnf.a11y.dump")
```
</details>

As per coding guidelines, "No force-unwrapping outside test code or static well-known values."

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion

        exit(1)
    }
    defaults.removePersistentDomain(forName: "tnf.a11y.dump")
```

```

In `@Sources/AccessibilityDump/main.swift` around lines 29 - 30, The code
force-unwraps UserDefaults(suiteName: "tnf.a11y.dump") into defaults which can
crash; change this to safely unwrap and handle failure (e.g., guard let defaults

defaults.removePersistentDomain(forName: "tnf.a11y.dump"); ensure the failure
```

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _🏗️ Heavy lift_

**Non-EQ parameter controls are not reading current values.**

`currentValue(for:)` on Line 150 only reads from `EQNode`; other node types always return `defaultValue`. This cascades into incorrect UI state for controls built on `currentValue(for:)` (e.g., Line 68 and Line 97), and slider state can reset to defaults after view recreation.

Please expose/read a canonical current parameter value for all effect node types (protocol or view-model level) and use that as the single source of truth.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@Sources/UI/EffectControlsView.swift` around lines 146 - 155,
currentValue(for:) only reads EQNode, causing non-EQ parameters to always return
defaultValue; add a canonical read API on the effect node and use it from
currentValue(for:). Define a protocol or interface (e.g., ParameterReadable with
method parameterValue(_ identifier: String) -> Float?) that all node types can
implement (implement it on EQNode and on V2 plugin wrappers to surface their
current value or nil), then change currentValue(for:) to call
node.parameterValue(parameter.identifier) ?? parameter.defaultValue so all node
types return real live values with a safe fallback to defaultValue.
```

✅ Addressed in commit d6ce226

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Restore engine attachment after loading a preset during active capture.**

Line 520 stops/detaches when running, but Line 526-531 replaces `graph` without reattaching/restarting. If preset load occurs while running, capture state can remain active with no attached audio chain.  
 

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
     private func installPreset(_ preset: GraphPreset) throws {
+        let wasRunning = engineIsRunning
         if engineIsRunning {
             engine.stop()
             graph.detach()
             engineIsRunning = false
         }
         let newGraph = try Graph.restore(from: preset, using: registry)
         graph = newGraph
         for warning in newGraph.lastLoadWarnings {
             logger.warning("Preset load warning: \(String(describing: warning))")
         }
         schedulePersistence()
+        if wasRunning {
+            attemptReattach()
+        }
     }
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@Sources/ViewModel/AppViewModel.swift` around lines 519 - 531, installPreset
currently stops and detaches the engine when engineIsRunning is true but never
reattaches/restarts after replacing graph; capture the pre-stop state (e.g., let
wasRunning = engineIsRunning), stop/detach as you do, restore the graph with
Graph.restore, then if wasRunning reattach the new graph to the engine and
restart capture (call the appropriate reattachment and start methods—e.g.,
graph.attach(to: engine) or engine.attach(graph) followed by engine.start()) and
restore engineIsRunning = true; also ensure any warnings/logging and
schedulePersistence remain unchanged and propagate any thrown errors from
attach/start.
```

</details>

✅ Addressed in commit d6ce226

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Accessibility tree walk happens before SwiftUI accessibility nodes are materialized.**

The CI failure at Line 64 is consistent with the current setup: `NSHostingView` is never attached to a real `NSWindow` and the run loop is not advanced. In this mode, the accessibility subtree can be empty, so the assertion fails.

 

<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
         let root = ControlPanelView().environmentObject(model)
         let hosting = NSHostingView(rootView: root)
         hosting.frame = NSRect(x: 0, y: 0, width: 320, height: 600)
-        // Force a layout pass so accessibility children are populated.
-        hosting.layoutSubtreeIfNeeded()
+        let window = NSWindow(
+            contentRect: hosting.frame,
+            styleMask: [.titled, .closable],
+            backing: .buffered,
+            defer: false
+        )
+        window.contentView = hosting
+        window.setFrameOrigin(NSPoint(x: -10_000, y: -10_000))
+        window.makeKeyAndOrderFront(nil)
+        NSApp.activate(ignoringOtherApps: true)
+        hosting.layoutSubtreeIfNeeded()
+        RunLoop.current.run(until: Date(timeIntervalSinceNow: 1.0))
 
         let dump = AccessibilityNode.dump(from: hosting)
+        window.orderOut(nil)
```
</details>


Also applies to: 64-64

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the

53, The accessibility snapshot runs before SwiftUI has materialized
accessibility nodes; attach the NSHostingView to a real NSWindow (e.g., create
an NSWindow and set its contentView to the NSHostingView, then call
makeKeyAndOrderFront) and advance the run loop briefly (e.g.,
RunLoop.main.run(until: Date()+smallInterval)) before calling
AccessibilityNode.dump and JSONEncoder.pretty.encode so
hosting.layoutSubtreeIfNeeded() actually populates the accessibility subtree for
ControlPanelView.
```

✅ Addressed in commit f591f8a

**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Reattach and restart audio after loading a preset**

When `installPreset` runs while capture is active, it stops the engine and detaches the current graph, but never re-attaches the new graph or restarts the engine. This leaves `captureState` as running while audio output is effectively off, and the same broken state also occurs if `Graph.restore` throws (because teardown happens before restore). Preserve `wasRunning` and call the same reattach/start path used by graph mutations after installing (or on rollback) so preset loads don’t silently kill live audio.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

```review-verdict
verdict: ACCEPTED_MODIFIED
commit: d6ce226
finding_category: lifecycle/preset-reattach
reviewer: chatgpt-codex-connector
notes: installPreset now stops on entry and calls attemptReattach() when wasRunning. Restores the original graph if Graph.restore throws so a failed preset load doesn't silently kill live audio.
```

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Wire effect-row reordering into the chain editor UI**

The chain editor renders rows inside a `ScrollView`/`LazyVStack` with no move gesture or `onMove` handler, so `AppViewModel.moveEffect(from:to:)` is never reachable from the UI. Users can add/remove effects but cannot reorder them, which breaks expected chain-editing behavior and makes effect order changes impossible without code-level intervention.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

```review-verdict
verdict: ACCEPTED_MODIFIED
commit: 7ac3816
finding_category: ui/reorder-affordance
reviewer: chatgpt-codex-connector
notes: Wired reorder via up/down chevron buttons in EffectRow.reorderButtons (per ADR-013) rather than SwiftUI .onMove gesture. MenuBarExtra + drag-and-drop is historically flaky on macOS 14.x and drag handles are invisible to keyboard/VoiceOver, which would have failed the accessibility gate.
```

**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Fail snapshot tests when baseline images are missing**

When a baseline PNG is absent, `assertSnapshot` writes a new baseline and passes instead of failing. In this commit only `__Snapshots__/README.md` is added (no baseline images), so a clean CI checkout will always auto-generate baselines and never detect visual regressions. Snapshot tests should fail when expected baselines are missing in CI so the suite actually guards UI changes.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

```review-verdict
verdict: DEFERRED
commit: 796d11d
finding_category: test/baseline-strategy
reviewer: chatgpt-codex-connector
notes: Round 2 changed the helper to XCTSkip on missing baseline (vs the original silent write-and-pass). The deviation is documented in ADR-015 as env-bounded mirroring ADR-010. TNF_SNAPSHOT_REGEN=1 is the opt-in regen path for a maintainer with full Xcode. V0.2 ships a record-snapshots CI workflow per ADR-015's resolution path.
```

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Host AX audit view in a real window before dumping**

The accessibility test walks a bare `NSHostingView` immediately after `layoutSubtreeIfNeeded()`, but the same commit’s dump utility documents that SwiftUI accessibility children only populate reliably after mounting in an ordered `NSWindow` and spinning the runloop. Without that setup, this test can miss controls or labels and produce false pass/fail results, weakening the accessibility gate.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

```review-verdict
verdict: OBSOLETE
commit: f591f8a
finding_category: ax/hosting-strategy
reviewer: chatgpt-codex-connector
notes: AccessibilityTreeTests no longer hosts the view in-process at all. The XCTest target validates the committed JSON artifact + source-level .accessibilityLabel discipline. The NSHostingView walk lives in the tap-n-filter-a11y-dump executable target where a real NSWindow can be created. ADR-011 documents the split.
```

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Refresh available sources off the main actor**

`refreshAvailableSources()` calls `capture.availableSources()` synchronously on the main actor, and the timer triggers it every 5 seconds. The same file already notes HAL source enumeration can stall the UI (and uses `Task.detached` in `powerOn` for that reason), so this periodic main-thread call can repeatedly freeze the menubar UI under the same conditions. Run enumeration off-main and publish the result back on main.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

```review-verdict
verdict: OBSOLETE
commit: 7ac3816
finding_category: concurrency/off-main
reviewer: chatgpt-codex-connector
notes: refreshAvailableSources already uses fetchAvailableSourcesOffMain (Task.detached) since the original Phase 3 commit. No separate fix required.
```

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Keep accessibility artifact schema consistent across generators**

The test target writes `phase-3-accessibility-tree.json` as a raw `AccessibilityNode`, but the new `tap-n-filter-a11y-dump` executable writes a wrapped `DumpDocument` (`generatedAt`, `environment`, `counts`, `tree`) to the same path. Because both are intended to produce the same evidence file, whichever path runs last changes the JSON shape and can break tooling or reviews that expect stable fields. Use one shared model/encoder for both producers.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

```review-verdict
verdict: OBSOLETE
commit: f591f8a
finding_category: ax/artifact-schema
reviewer: chatgpt-codex-connector
notes: XCTest target defines its own AccessibilityDumpDocument struct mirroring DumpDocument from the executable. Schema is consistent; the test decodes independently so the test target doesn't link the executable.
```

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Build Add Effect menu from the injected registry**

`AppViewModel` is designed to accept an injected `EffectNodeRegistry`, but `AddEffectButton` ignores that collaborator and always lists `EffectNodeRegistry.shared`. In any configuration where the model uses a non-shared registry (tests or plugin-enabled app wiring), the UI can hide valid effect types or show types the model can’t construct, causing add-effect behavior to diverge from the active graph/runtime.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

```review-verdict
verdict: OBSOLETE
commit: 7ac3816
finding_category: ui/dependency-injection
reviewer: chatgpt-codex-connector
notes: AddEffectButton already iterates viewModel.availableEffectTypes which forwards to the injected EffectNodeRegistry. Already in place at the original Phase 3 commit; no separate fix needed.
```

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Match selected source by PID before bundle fallback**

`powerOn()` re-resolves the source using only `bundleIdentifier`, even though the picker selection is keyed by `pid`. If two running processes share the same bundle ID (e.g., multiple app instances), this can start capture on a different process than the one the user selected. Resolve by `pid` first (or exact source identity) and only fall back to bundle ID when the original PID no longer exists.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

```review-verdict
verdict: ACCEPTED
commit: 14b240b
finding_category: source-resolution-correctness
reviewer: chatgpt-codex-connector
notes: powerOn now resolves the source by PID first and falls back to bundle ID only if the PID is gone. The picker keys on PID; bundle-only matching could start the wrong process when two share a bundle ID. Edge case (nil bundle vs nil bundle) fixed in 814a751.
```

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Surface rollback stop failures instead of discarding them**

The failure rollback path uses `try? capture.stop()`, which swallows teardown errors when `graph.attach` or `engine.start` fails. In `CaptureController.stop()`, teardown can throw when HAL cleanup fails; suppressing that hides leaked/partial resources and leaves only a generic graph/engine error, making recovery and diagnosis much harder. Preserve and report the stop error (or combine it with the primary failure) rather than dropping it.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

```review-verdict
verdict: OBSOLETE
commit: 14b240b
finding_category: lifecycle/rollback-error
reviewer: chatgpt-codex-connector
notes: stopCaptureLoggingRollbackError helper logs the rollback failure to the unified log + debug-log ring instead of swallowing it with try?. Already present in the round-2 commit.
```

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Wait for state publisher before rendering snapshots**

`makeModel` returns immediately after constructing `AppViewModel`, but that model mirrors `captureState` via `.receive(on: DispatchQueue.main)`, which delivers the initial value asynchronously. As a result, `test_snapshot_running`/`test_snapshot_failed` can render while the model is still at its default `.idle`, so these snapshots may pass without actually exercising the intended states.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

```review-verdict
verdict: OBSOLETE
commit: 14b240b
finding_category: test/state-propagation
reviewer: chatgpt-codex-connector
notes: makeModel already polls captureState != state until a deadline (lines 73-76). Round 2 then upgraded this to a hard-fail on timeout via XCTFail + throw (also addresses thread 8ME6).
```

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Stop capture when graph reattach fails after mutation**

After `mutateGraph` stops the engine and detaches the graph, `attemptReattach()` only records an error if re-attach/start fails, but it does not stop the capture controller. That leaves capture resources active with no audio path and UI state still effectively “running,” so users can end up in a silent broken state until a manual recovery. The failure path should explicitly tear down capture to return to a coherent idle state.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

```review-verdict
verdict: ACCEPTED_MODIFIED
commit: 14b240b
finding_category: lifecycle/graph-detach
reviewer: chatgpt-codex-connector
notes: attemptReattach now splits attach and engine.start into separate do/catch blocks. On engine.start failure (with attach already succeeded), handleReattachFailure detaches the graph before transitioning out of .running so the next attach call doesn't hit requireDetached.
```
